[{'date': '2026-05-14', 'rating': 1, 'country': 'US', 'title': 'Language unacceptable', 'content': 'I hate that curse words are often included in my affirmations that I receive and I cannot block it. It makes me not open the app because this is not how I speak to myself. I don’t know why this changed. This app should be better.', 'author': 'badst11', 'version': '6.36.0'}, {'date': '2026-05-13', 'rating': 2, 'country': 'US', 'title': 'Misleading Offer and Cost', 'content': 'Like others, I downloaded this app with the idea of trying it out before deciding if it was worth my time. Upon opening for the first time, there was about 10 minutes worth of questions I had to answer. As a customer who is curious about the product, getting barraged with personal questions before I can see what this product even is is already a big negative. Like most other companies on the internet, I assume they’re mining my data almost immediately (which is NOT a good feeling). 
Then my biggest problem is the subscription situation. After all those questions I get hit that it’s $70 for the year. There was no explanation on what that entailed (and again I hadn’t even made it to the interface of the actual app yet) and no other subscription options (which turns out there are?!?). They’re lucky I was still curious so I continued, thinking I will annoyingly have to make more time in my schedule just to cancel the subscription, again having to promise my time to something I don’t even know about. Inside the app, I find it mostly interesting and functional. The buttons on the Home Screen are a little confusing on what takes you where. 
Then I go to the subscription section to see what’s going on, thinking I’m going to cancel because $70 is too much. Doing that through Apple brought me to a page with the other options… finally. There are 4 options, none of which have any real explanation of what they are or do other than how much you’re charged per month/year. I notice a $35/year option….what does that entail? Why was that not presented to me in the beginning? How is that different $70? I literally have no way of knowing what that option actually comes with. 
Ultimately the interface is beautiful, the idea is great and truly almost there. But the elusiveness and lack of transparency around billing and subscriptions leaves a very bad taste. On to find a similar app without all the upfront hassle.', 'author': 'Msdetective', 'version': '6.36.0'}, {'date': '2026-05-13', 'rating': 5, 'country': 'US', 'title': 'Simply Powerful', 'content': 'Using “I am - Daily Affirmations” is a simply powerful way to orient to a healthy mindset and individual wellbeing. I love the affirmations provided by the app and being able to write my own. Always a great part of my day!', 'author': 'Shan M. These daily affirmations has given me hope to press forward in persevere.', 'author': 'Royalty 12', 'version': '6.35.0'}, {'date': '2026-05-12', 'rating': 4, 'country': 'US', 'title': 'Tons of updates & improvements, and Developers are on top of improving glitches fast!', 'content': 'Currently there’s a Wealth of improvements going on in this app making it fun and enjoyable for numerous age ranges and numerous people’s needs. The app has beautiful graphic images and much more readability in the text on the images for those who have lower vision. There’s numerous ways to customize this app to fit your needs which I truly enjoy. I write my own affirmations and do my own customized image design in the app. The app now includes an AI affirmations writer for those who have things they want to work on AI will customize 10-16 affirmations per day if needed for you. It’s actually a very fun and useful tool. 


Unfortunately the last 2 updates have a little glitch that the developers are working on right now. With all the beautiful new features they have added. And a wonderful new event experience for mental health month…the ability to edit your own affirmations or the words of pre-written affirmations has disappeared from the app. Also the ability to edit your own categories has disappeared. 

But the app developers are aware of this little glitch which actually had numerous other hiccups 3 weeks ago. The developers are indeed on it. They really do care about the apps they create for us to use. They have been in very close contact with me, and they have been fixing all the little hiccups. 

Trust me when I say that what seems like a tiny annoyance to us is actually a huge problem for app developers. They have to go through numerous changes and make sure that the app not only functions and does what they want it to do and it doesn’t do something that they don’t want. But then they have to ensure that it’s coding and protocols meet all the rules of every single platform and operating system that can use the app. It’s really a lot of work for the app developers. And for them to be this on top of it. 

Definitely deserves a ton of credit. 4 out of 5 stars only because there’s the little glitch that the developers are working on as I’m typing. 

Will definitely be a 5-7stars which they don’t allow more than 5 stars but they should. For this app. Because they should allow the 2 stars for customer support so like other things 5 stars if everything is good 6 stars if customer service included with professionalism. 7 stars if treated with respect and made to feel important and valued. But you know only penthouses and restaurants get this star rating. Not apps. But when the apps catch up to the ratings they deserve this would be one of those 7 stars. Because they have a great development team.

If you’re needing a daily boost, I highly recommend this app.', 'author': 'Missrum29', 'version': '6.33.0'}, {'date': '2026-04-22', 'rating': 2, 'country': 'AU', 'title': 'The app may be amazing but ..', 'content': 'This app may be amazing but it sets an alarm that can be deleted even if the app was deleted. So I didn’t love it and decided not to continue with the pro version, I now have a permanent alarm at 7:55am every day that can’t turn off :(', 'author': 'gadelichka', 'version': '6.32.0'}, {'date': '2026-04-21', 'rating': 3, 'country': 'US', 'title': 'Amazing app until they started adding pop ups.', 'content': 'I paid a year subscription for this app and have found it well worth it. It’s grounding, peaceful, and aligning. I love that you can choose your own background photo or video. I use ocean waves and it includes the sound in the background. It’s perfect. I like to scroll through once a day and “heart” my favourite affirmations.

Recently they started adding pop ups as you scroll through. When I “heart” an affirmation it brings up a pop up telling me to share with a friend, that I then have to close to continue. And it doesn’t just come up once, but continuously. This seems like a small thing, but it seriously disturbs the meditative flow. And having what is basically an in app advertisement “share our app!” pop up so consistently on a paid app, is pretty disappointing and leads me to not enjoy the app as much. 

I appreciate the work put into this app. I hope the creators can understand is this is counter to the point of the app, and find better ways to encourage us to share.
